section: HALL ADMISSION, SEAT ALLOCATION & RENEWAL POLICY
text: Hall Admission is open to full-time students of DIU. Preference will be given to out-station students and newly admitted students.

section: HALL ADMISSION, SEAT ALLOCATION & RENEWAL POLICY
text: Students can book a seat either online or by submitting an application to the respective Hall Office during the office hours for manual booking. Incomplete or providing false information in application will be canceled

section: HALL ADMISSION, SEAT ALLOCATION & RENEWAL POLICY
text: 5 to retain their seat. If a student fails to meet this requirement, their seat may be cancelled, or the Provost may take appropriate action as deemed necessary.

section: HALL ADMISSION, SEAT ALLOCATION & RENEWAL POLICY
text: Selected applicants for Hall admission will be notified via letter, email, or a notice on the university website at least three (03) days before the semester begins. Once the seat is confirmed, the applicant must occupy the accommodation within the given deadline. Additionally, the required payment must be completed within three (03) working days of receiving the confirmation. Failure to do so will result in cancellation of the allotment and the application will be considered invalid.

section: HALL ADMISSION, SEAT ALLOCATION & RENEWAL POLICY
text: Hall admission is valid for one academic semester only. To continue for the next semester, students must renew their seat through the online portal and submit payment confirmation by the scheduled deadline. When circulated by the Hall authority, students must also provide their consent to continue using the Hall facilities. Additionally, students must maintain a minimum CGPA of

section: HALL ADMISSION, SEAT ALLOCATION & RENEWAL POLICY
text: Students must apply for seat cancellation for the next semester by the specified deadline and clear all outstanding dues before doing so. A penalty of Tk. 1000 will be charged for late cancellations. After cancellation, the student must vacate the hall and return the room and all allotted items to the Hall Office in proper condition.

section: HALL ADMISSION, SEAT ALLOCATION & RENEWAL POLICY
text: Refund policy: Refund of hall fees is allowed only upon cancellation of DIU admission. Tk. 3000/- will be deducted for processing charges if canceled before classes start; no refund is available after commencement of class or after the accommodation period begins.

section: HALL ADMISSION, SEAT ALLOCATION & RENEWAL POLICY
text: No student will be allowed to continue occupying a hall seat if he/she discontinues academic activities or fails to register for the ongoing academic semester. In such cases, the hall seat will be subject to cancellation. If a student needs to withdraw from an academic semester for valid and documented reasons, he/she must seek prior approval from the Provost by submitting a formal application to request permission to continue residing in the Hall. Furthermore, the student is required to pay the Hall fees by the specified payment deadline.

section: HALL ADMISSION, SEAT ALLOCATION & RENEWAL POLICY
text: The hall must be vacated with all personal belongings during semester breaks or extended leaves such as Eid, Puja, or other religious festivals. Students are solely responsible for their valuables, including mobile phones, laptops, personal computers, or cash, and must carry them at their own risk.

section: HALL ADMISSION, SEAT ALLOCATION & RENEWAL POLICY
text: The Hall fee for the next academic semester is required to be paid during the seat renewal schedule or before. late fee of Tk. 500 will be charged for payments made after the due date (for seat renewal or semester payment).

section: HALL ADMISSION, SEAT ALLOCATION & RENEWAL POLICY
text: Room or Hall change without the prior consent of the Provost is strictly prohibited. Any student wishing to change their room or hall must submit a formal application to the Provost and, upon approval, pay a fee of Tk. 3000 as room/hall change charges. However, if the Provost finds the reason for the change to be justified and reasonable, the fee may be waived at the Provost’s discretion.

section: HALL ADMISSION, SEAT ALLOCATION & RENEWAL POLICY
text: Creative International (CI) management reserves the right to revise hall fees structure when deemed necessary, with prior notice provided to all concerned parties.

Visitors are not permitted to enter student rooms. They must be received in the Visitors’ Room or Reception Area between 9:00 a.m. and 8:00 p.m. Guest accommodation may be allowed with prior written approval from the Provost. Note: Female guests are not allowed in the Boys’ Halls, and male guests are not allowed in the Female halls.

Every male student must return to their respective Hall by 10:00 PM. Female students must return by 6:00 PM (November to February) and 7:00 PM (March to October). If any student needs to stay outside the Hall beyond the specified time due to a valid reason, they must obtain prior written permission from the Provost through the respective House Tutor. Failure to secure prior permission will result in disciplinary action, including penalties or possible expulsion from the Hall

Students must seek and obtain prior permission from the Provost for any night outs, which are allowed only for visits to the local guardian or parents' house. Permission should be requested at least one day in advance.

The Hall authority retains the right to enter and inspect any room at any time, with or without prior notice, including when the student is not present. This may occur especially in cases of emergency, security concerns, or maintenance requirements.
The Hall Authority shall not be held responsible for any mishap occurring outside the hall premises.
During their stay in the Hall, they will be under the Supervision of the respective Hall Provost.

All matters related to conflicts among students or allegations against Hall staff, must be reported to the Provost who will take appropriate action as deemed necessary. No student is permitted to lodge a police complaint without obtaining prior permission from the Provost.
The Hall Authority will decide the allocation of rooms / seats, furniture, shoe racks, and hangers. Students are not allowed to bring or use any personal furniture in their hall rooms.

The Hall Authority is not responsible for the safety of students' personal belongings. Students are advised to use their own locks and take proper care of their possessions. They must not leave their room keys unattended or in insecure places. Any loss of personal items will be the sole responsibility of the student.

section: HEALTH, ISSUE AND STAFF CONDUCT GUIDELINES
text: Any case of illness, accident, or emergency must be reported immediately to the Hall Office or to the designated staff on duty that day.

section: HEALTH, ISSUE AND STAFF CONDUCT GUIDELINES
text: Students diagnosed with any contagious disease will not be permitted to stay in the Hall. The decision of the Provost in such matters will be final and must be strictly followed.

section: HEALTH, ISSUE AND STAFF CONDUCT GUIDELINES
text: Any complaint, suggestion, or feedback regarding hall services must be submitted to the Hall Authority through the proper channels. Sharing such matters on social media is strictly prohibited.

section: HEALTH, ISSUE AND STAFF CONDUCT GUIDELINES
text: No students shall use the service of a Hall staff for personal work even on payment. He/she shall also not bring any staff from outside even temporarily.

Smoking, the consumption of alcoholic drinks or drugs, use of e-cigarettes, and the selling or trading of drugs are strictly prohibited both within the hall and across the campus, as we uphold a 100% tobacco-free environment. Violation of this rule will result in strict disciplinary action, temporary suspension from the hall, or permanent expulsion from the university, depending on the severity of the offense.

If any student is found engaging in misbehavior, misconduct, ragging, body shaming, bullying, harassment, discrimination, or intimidation, he/she will be immediately expelled from the Hall, and any fees paid will be forfeited. Violators will also face disciplinary actions such as warnings or temporary suspension depending on the severity of the offense.
Residents should not participate in any anti-national, anti-social or undesirable activity in or outside the campus.
If any individual is found engaged in any sexual activities, his/her hall seat will be immediately canceled. A report will be submitted to the Proctor's Office for further action.

Unlawful protests, shouting, mob gatherings, or any attempt to incite unrest within the hall or campus premises are strictly prohibited. Students are expected to voice their concerns through proper channels, such as the complaint box, support ticket system, hall officer, House Tutor, or the Provost. Any violation of this policy will lead to disciplinary action, which may include a written warning, guardian notification, temporary suspension from the hall, or permanent cancellation of hall allotment, depending on the severity of the offense.
All forms of sports or physical games are strictly prohibited inside hall rooms and in any areas not specifically designated for such activities.

No student is allowed to disturb others by playing musical instruments, using loudspeakers, watching videos or playing games at high volume, shouting, or engaging in any disruptive activities that may affect the peace and discipline of the hall. Any such behavior will be treated as a violation of hall rules and may lead to disciplinary action.

The use of any heavy electrical appliances is strictly prohibited. Examples include heaters, cookers, irons, washing machines, microwave ovens, room heaters, coolers, freezers, and similar devices. However, students may avail microwave oven and freezer services provided by the Hall office.

Each student is responsible for maintaining the cleanliness and tidiness of their allocated room at all times. They must also ensure the proper use and care of the furniture and fixtures provided to them. Posting posters, leaflets, banners, or similar items on walls, gates, or in lobbies is strictly prohibited.
Students are not allowed to enter the other students’ room without permission of the inmates.

Resident students are strictly prohibited from convening any type of meeting within the Hall premises without prior approval from the Provost. Similarly, no functions or celebrations may be organized without the Provost’s prior permission.

Any damage to hall property, including furniture and fixtures, caused by a student’s negligence will be charged to the student or the group of students residing in the concerned room. Any damage to hall property, including furniture and fixtures, resulting from a student’s negligence will be the responsibility of the student or the group of students occupying the affected room. The cost of repairs or replacement will be charged to them accordingly.
Students are expected to switch off lights, fans, and other electrical appliances when leaving their rooms and are encouraged to take necessary precautions to conserve electricity

Students are prohibited from driving nails, screws, or similar objects into walls or doors. They are not allowed to attempt any repairs themselves. If any repairs are needed, students must report the issue to the Hall Office, which will arrange for the necessary repairs to be carried out.

All facilities, including additional amenities such as TV, refrigerator, oven, magazines, newspapers, and internet, will be withdrawn without prior notice if misused. Disciplinary action will be taken against the students responsible for the misuse.

Students must lodge any dining-related complaints immediately at the hall office after consuming the food in the dining hall. Complaints will not be accepted if the food is taken to the room or consumed elsewhere. If unresolved, the issue may be escalated to the hall management authority by maintaining the proper chain of command. Complaints made directly to higher authorities, bypassing the hall office or hall management, will not be entertained.

Power Outages and Generator Failures: Students must remain calm, composed, and cooperative during any unexpected power failure, generator malfunction, or technical fault. Misuse of electrical equipment, tampering with facilities, or interfering with maintenance operations is strictly prohibited and will be treated as a violation of hall discipline.

Water Pump and Lift Malfunctions: In case of water supply interruptions or lift malfunctions, students must act patiently and responsibly without creating panic. Hall students should cooperate with hall authorities during maintenance (Plumbing, Electrical, Furniture, Internet, Generator, etc.) or repair work. Spreading misinformation, rumors, or unnecessary complaints during technical issues is strictly discouraged and may result in disciplinary action.

section: COMPLIANCE WITH HALL RULES & ADMINISTRATIVE RIGHTS
text: Students residing in the Hall are required to strictly follow all rules and regulations set by the Hall Authority. The Hall Authority also reserves the absolute right to cancel the admission or allotment of any student found to be undeserving or unfit for residence, without the obligation to provide any explanation or justification. ***Important Note: All students are expected to uphold mutual respect, patience, and discipline during any form of technical disruption within the residential facilities.
